evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Methods used (stripe.Account.create with type=express, stripe.AccountLink.create, stripe.PaymentIntent.create with application_fee_amount + transfer_data.destination) are internally consistent and match the documented Connect Express destination-charge pattern. No deprecated traps matched. No explicit version date stated though stripe>=8.0 pinned.
evidence.executability: All fenced blocks are syntactically valid Python/JSX. Imports present. Publishable key is a placeholder ('pk_test_12345...') the extractor will write verbatim — frontend will fail to load Stripe until replaced, but this is a config value not a code bug. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/brew/sudo).
evidence.completeness: (a) Key acquisition URL+menu ✓; (b) install bash ✓; (c) .env template ✓ (STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, FRONTEND_URL — matches backend references); (d) backend code ✓; (e) frontend code ✓; (f) test procedure is manual steps, not a runnable test command in a bash block — partial miss. No webhook handling for account.updated or payment_intent.succeeded.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Only one link present (dashboard.stripe.com) which is the dashboard, not docs.stripe.com documentation. No anchoring of code to official docs URLs.

rubric_notes: Test section uses manual steps rather than an extractable bash test command; no webhook signature verification shown.
